title: First Day at a New High School

all.en: Emily stood nervously at the school gate, clutching the strap of her brand new backpack.
all.zh: 艾蜜莉緊張地站在校門口，緊握著她全新背包的背帶。

all.en: Her family had moved from a small town, and everything in this big city school felt overwhelming.
all.zh: 她的家人從一個小鎮搬來，這所大城市學校裡的一切都讓她感到難以承受。

all.en: She took a deep breath and walked through the gate, trying to look more confident than she felt.
all.zh: 她深吸一口氣，走過大門，努力讓自己看起來比實際感受更有自信。

all.en: A friendly girl with glasses noticed her confused expression and offered to show her around.
all.zh: 一位戴著眼鏡的友善女孩注意到她困惑的表情，主動提議帶她四處看看。

all.en: Her name was Mia, and she explained where the classrooms, library, and cafeteria were located.
all.zh: 她的名字叫米雅，她解釋了教室、圖書館和餐廳的位置。

all.en: During homeroom, the teacher introduced Emily to the class and asked her to share a hobby.
all.zh: 在導師時間，老師將艾蜜莉介紹給全班，並請她分享一項興趣愛好。

all.en: Emily blushed and quietly said that she enjoyed painting watercolors in her free time.
all.zh: 艾蜜莉臉紅了，輕聲說她喜歡在閒暇時畫水彩畫。

all.en: Two students immediately invited her to join the art club after school that day.
all.zh: 兩位學生立刻邀請她當天放學後加入美術社。

all.en: By lunchtime, Emily was already laughing with Mia and her group of friends over sandwiches.
all.zh: 到了午餐時間，艾蜜莉已經在三明治旁與米雅和她的朋友們一起歡笑了。

all.en: She realized that making friends was not as difficult as she had imagined the night before.
all.zh: 她意識到交朋友並沒有她前一晚想像的那麼困難。

all.en: When the final bell rang, Emily walked home with a small smile and a sense of belonging.
all.zh: 當放學鐘聲響起時，艾蜜莉帶著淡淡的微笑和歸屬感走回家。

all.en: The next morning, Emily woke up feeling much less anxious about going to school.
all.zh: 隔天早晨，艾蜜莉醒來時對上學的焦慮感少了許多。

all.en: She packed her sketchbook and watercolor set, hoping to use them at the art club.
all.zh: 她打包了素描本和水彩組，希望能在美術社用上它們。

all.en: Mia was waiting near the school gate and waved enthusiastically when Emily arrived.
all.zh: 米雅在校門附近等著，艾蜜莉到達時她熱情地揮手。

all.en: During English class, the teacher praised Emily's pronunciation and clear writing style.
all.zh: 在英文課上，老師稱讚了艾蜜莉的發音和清晰的寫作風格。

all.en: At lunch, she met a quiet boy named Ethan who also loved drawing fantasy creatures.
all.zh: 午餐時，她認識了一個叫伊森的安靜男孩，他也喜歡畫奇幻生物。

all.en: They quickly exchanged drawing tips and even traded a few rough sketches on napkins.
all.zh: 他們很快交換了繪畫技巧，甚至在餐巾紙上交換了幾張粗略的草圖。

all.en: After school, the art club teacher gave Emily a warm welcome and a fresh canvas.
all.zh: 放學後，美術社老師熱情歡迎艾蜜莉，並給了她一塊新畫布。

all.en: She painted a peaceful river scene inspired by the small town she had left behind.
all.zh: 她畫了一幅寧靜的河流景色，靈感來自於她離開的那個小鎮。

all.en: Several club members gathered around her easel, admiring the soft blue and green colors.
all.zh: 幾位社員圍在她的畫架旁，欣賞那柔和的藍色與綠色。

all.en: That evening, Emily told her mother that the new city already felt a little like home.
all.zh: 那天晚上，艾蜜莉告訴媽媽，這座新城市已經有點像家了。

all.en: The following week, Emily and Ethan started planning a small comic book together during lunch breaks.
all.zh: 接下來那週，艾蜜莉和伊森開始在午休時間一起規劃一本小型漫畫書。

all.en: Mia helped them brainstorm story ideas based on a magical forest filled with talking animals.
all.zh: 米雅幫他們腦力激盪，發想一個充滿會說話動物的魔法森林故事。

all.en: Emily designed the main character, a brave young fox with a sparkling silver tail.
all.zh: 艾蜜莉設計了主角，一隻擁有閃亮銀色尾巴的勇敢小狐狸。

all.en: Ethan focused on the villain, a clever raven who collected stolen jewels in a hidden cave.
all.zh: 伊森則專注於反派角色，一隻在隱密洞穴中收藏贓寶的聰明烏鴉。

all.en: They decided to enter their finished comic into the school's annual creative arts competition.
all.zh: 他們決定把完成的漫畫投稿到學校的年度創意藝術競賽。

all.en: The art teacher kindly offered to review their pages and gave thoughtful suggestions for improvement.
all.zh: 美術老師親切地提出檢視他們的稿件，並給出貼心的改進建議。

all.en: Emily's mother bought her a new set of professional drawing pens as a surprise gift.
all.zh: 艾蜜莉的媽媽送她一套全新的專業繪圖筆作為驚喜禮物。

all.en: They worked tirelessly every weekend, often staying at the public library until closing time.
all.zh: 他們每個週末都不知疲倦地創作，常待在公共圖書館直到閉館。

all.en: On the day of the competition, Emily nervously handed in their twenty-page colorful comic book.
all.zh: 比賽當天，艾蜜莉緊張地遞交了他們二十頁的彩色漫畫書。

all.en: Two weeks later, they were thrilled to learn that their creation had won first place.
all.zh: 兩週後，他們欣喜地得知他們的作品獲得了第一名。

all.en: The local newspaper published a short article about their award-winning comic book creation.
all.zh: 當地報紙刊登了一篇關於他們得獎漫畫作品的短篇文章。

all.en: Emily's photograph appeared on the front page beside Ethan and their proud art teacher.
all.zh: 艾蜜莉的照片刊登在頭版，旁邊是伊森和他們驕傲的美術老師。

all.en: Several younger students approached her in the hallway, asking for autographs and drawing tips.
all.zh: 幾位低年級學生在走廊上走向她，請她簽名並請教繪畫技巧。

all.en: Emily felt deeply moved by their admiration and decided to organize a free weekly art workshop.
all.zh: 艾蜜莉被他們的欣賞深深感動，決定舉辦一個免費的每週藝術工作坊。

all.en: Mia volunteered to help manage the workshop schedule and prepare colorful sample materials.
all.zh: 米雅自願協助安排工作坊的時間表，並準備色彩豐富的範例材料。

all.en: Ethan offered to teach character design using simple shapes and easy step-by-step techniques.
all.zh: 伊森提議教大家用簡單的形狀和容易上手的逐步技巧設計角色。

all.en: The workshop quickly attracted more than twenty enthusiastic beginners from different grade levels.
all.zh: 這個工作坊很快吸引了超過二十位來自不同年級充滿熱情的初學者。

all.en: Emily's mother donated brand new sketchbooks and pencils to support the growing creative community.
all.zh: 艾蜜莉的媽媽捐出全新的素描本和鉛筆，以支持這個日益茁壯的創意社群。

all.en: After several months, the students hosted a small exhibition in the school's main entrance hall.
all.zh: 幾個月後，學生們在學校大門入口處舉辦了一場小型展覽。

all.en: Emily realized that the new city she once feared had become her true creative home.
all.zh: 艾蜜莉意識到，她曾經害怕的這座新城市，已成為她真正的創意家園。

all.en: During the spring semester, Emily was invited to display her art at the city library exhibit.
all.zh: 春季學期，艾蜜莉受邀在市立圖書館展覽中展示她的作品。

all.en: She prepared five large watercolor paintings depicting peaceful scenes from her childhood town.
all.zh: 她準備了五幅大型水彩畫，描繪她童年小鎮的寧靜景色。

all.en: Mia and Ethan helped her transport the heavy frames carefully on the city bus that morning.
all.zh: 那天早晨，米雅和伊森幫她小心地用市區公車運送沉重的畫框。

all.en: The librarian welcomed them warmly and helped arrange the paintings under perfect lighting.
all.zh: 圖書館員熱情地歡迎他們，並協助把畫作擺放在完美的燈光下。

all.en: On opening night, dozens of curious visitors gathered to admire her colorful artistic talents.
all.zh: 開幕之夜，數十位好奇的訪客聚集起來欣賞她色彩繽紛的藝術才華。

all.en: A local newspaper reporter interviewed Emily about her creative inspiration and daily routines.
all.zh: 一位當地報社記者採訪了艾蜜莉，詢問她的創作靈感和日常作息。

all.en: Several visitors purchased prints to support her dream of attending a famous art university.
all.zh: 幾位訪客購買了畫作的複製品，支持她進入知名藝術大學的夢想。

all.en: Emily's mother watched silently from the corner, her eyes shining with pride and quiet love.
all.zh: 艾蜜莉的媽媽從角落靜靜地看著，眼中閃爍著驕傲與深沉的愛意。

all.en: After the exhibit ended, Emily donated half of her earnings to the school's art supply fund.
all.zh: 展覽結束後，艾蜜莉將一半的收入捐給學校的美術用品基金。

all.en: She realized her quiet passion had grown into something that could brighten the entire community.
all.zh: 她意識到自己安靜的熱情已成長為能照亮整個社區的力量。

all.en: Emily decided to use her remaining earnings to host free weekend art classes for younger neighborhood children.
all.zh: 艾蜜莉決定把剩下的收入用來為社區年幼的孩子們開設免費的週末美術課。

all.en: Her mother offered to set up a quiet corner of their home for these new Saturday art sessions.
all.zh: 媽媽自願在家中佈置一個安靜的角落，做為這些新的週六美術課場地。

all.en: Five children attended the first class, painting cheerful sunflowers under Emily's gentle guidance.
all.zh: 第一堂課有五位孩子前來，在艾蜜莉溫柔的指導下畫出快樂的向日葵。

all.en: Word spread quickly, and within a month, twelve students filled the small living room every weekend.
all.zh: 消息傳得很快，一個月內就有十二位學生擠滿了小客廳的每個週末。

all.en: Emily's father built a long sturdy table so every child could work comfortably without bumping elbows.
all.zh: 艾蜜莉的爸爸做了一張長而堅固的桌子，讓每個孩子都能舒適地畫畫不會撞到手肘。

all.en: One of the youngest students gave Emily a hand-drawn portrait, calling her the kindest art teacher ever.
all.zh: 其中最小的學生送了艾蜜莉一張手繪肖像，稱她是有史以來最溫柔的美術老師。

all.en: Emily framed the portrait and hung it above her own easel as a daily source of motivation.
all.zh: 艾蜜莉把那張肖像裱框後掛在自己的畫架上方，做為每天的動力來源。

all.en: When the art university acceptance letter finally arrived, Emily cried while her mother quietly clapped behind her.
all.zh: 美術大學的錄取通知終於寄到時，艾蜜莉哭了，媽媽就站在她身後輕輕鼓掌。

all.en: Emily promised her young students that she would still return on weekends, even during her busy semesters.
all.zh: 艾蜜莉向她的小學生們承諾，即使學期再忙，她依然會在週末回來上課。

all.en: She realized her shy beginnings at the new high school had grown into a calling that filled her heart.
all.zh: 她體會到自己剛轉學時的羞澀，已經成長為一份充滿她心房的使命。

all.en: During her first month at university, Emily quickly made new friends in her watercolor specialty studio.
all.zh: 進入大學的第一個月，艾蜜莉很快在她的水彩專業工作室裡結識了新朋友。

all.en: Every Friday evening, she took the bus home so she could prepare materials for Saturday's neighborhood class.
all.zh: 每個週五傍晚，她都會搭公車回家，為週六的社區美術課準備材料。

all.en: Her young students cheered loudly when she walked in, and they showed her every drawing they had made.
all.zh: 她走進教室時，小學生們都熱烈歡呼，向她展示自己這週畫的每一張畫。

all.en: Mia visited from another university across town and helped Emily organize a small spring art festival together.
all.zh: 米雅從城市另一端的大學過來，和艾蜜莉一起籌辦了一場小型的春季藝術節。

all.en: Ethan painted huge backdrop banners for the festival, attracting curious passersby from the entire neighborhood.
all.zh: 伊森為藝術節繪製了巨幅背景旗幟，吸引了整個社區的好奇路人駐足觀看。

all.en: Over forty children participated, painting tiny landscapes on smooth river stones from the nearby creek.
all.zh: 超過四十個孩子參與其中，在附近溪邊撿來的光滑石頭上畫出小巧的風景。

all.en: Emily arranged the painted stones into a winding garden path leading to her parents' front door.
all.zh: 艾蜜莉把畫好的石頭排成一條蜿蜒的花園小徑，通往她父母家的前門。

all.en: Her father proudly named the path Emily's Stone Trail and welcomed neighbors to admire it each evening.
all.zh: 她爸爸驕傲地把這條小徑命名為「艾蜜莉的石頭小徑」，每晚都歡迎鄰居前來欣賞。

all.en: One quiet evening, a stranger left a thank-you note among the stones, saying the trail had brightened her week.
all.zh: 某個寧靜的傍晚，一位陌生人在石頭間留下感謝紙條，說這條小徑點亮了她整週的心情。

all.en: Emily kept that note inside her sketchbook, knowing her quiet first day at this school had truly changed her life.
all.zh: 艾蜜莉把那張紙條夾在素描本裡，明白她在這所學校安靜的第一天，真的改變了她的人生。

all.en: By summer, Emily's neighborhood art class had grown so large that her parents converted the garage into a studio.
all.zh: 夏天時，艾蜜莉的社區美術課已經擴大到爸媽把車庫改建成一間工作室。

all.en: Dad installed bright overhead lighting and built five sturdy easels along the studio's freshly painted white walls.
all.zh: 爸爸裝上明亮的吸頂燈，並沿著工作室新粉刷的白牆做了五個堅固的畫架。

all.en: Mom sewed colorful aprons for every young student, embroidering each child's name in cheerful thread.
all.zh: 媽媽為每個小學生縫製了彩色圍裙，並用歡快的繡線在上面繡上每個孩子的名字。

all.en: Ethan moved to the studio neighborhood and visited every Saturday to teach a special character design hour.
all.zh: 伊森搬到工作室所在的社區，每個星期六都過來教一小時的角色設計專題課。

all.en: Mia helped Emily host a small online gallery so distant relatives could admire every student's recent painting.
all.zh: 米雅協助艾蜜莉建立一個小型線上畫廊，讓遠方的親戚也能欣賞每位學生最近的作品。

all.en: An older artist from the city offered to mentor Emily during her summer break from the university.
all.zh: 城裡一位年長的藝術家在艾蜜莉大學暑假期間，主動提議擔任她的導師。

all.en: The mentor taught her how to mix unusual color palettes that captured the warm feeling of late afternoon light.
all.zh: 導師教她調配與眾不同的色彩，能捕捉午後微光那份溫暖的氛圍。

all.en: Emily applied her new skills to a personal series of paintings about the small town she had once left behind.
all.zh: 艾蜜莉將新學到的技巧運用到一個個人系列創作上，主題是她曾經離開的那座小鎮。

all.en: The series was selected for display at a major regional museum, and her family attended every single opening event.
all.zh: 這個系列被選為一間區域大型美術館的展品，全家人出席了每一場開幕活動。

all.en: On the closing night, Emily smiled quietly, knowing every brushstroke had begun at one nervous school gate years ago.
all.zh: 閉幕之夜，艾蜜莉靜靜地微笑，明白每一筆畫都始於多年前那一道緊張的校門口。

all.en: The day after the museum exhibit closed, Emily spilled an entire jar of cobalt blue paint on her favorite jeans.
all.zh: 美術館展覽閉幕的隔天，艾蜜莉把一整罐鈷藍顏料打翻在她最愛的牛仔褲上。

all.en: Mom suggested keeping the jeans as a wearable memory, and Emily ended up turning them into her signature painting uniform.
all.zh: 媽媽建議把那條牛仔褲留下來當作可以穿在身上的回憶，艾蜜莉後來把它變成她招牌的作畫服。

all.en: Mia called from her university to say she had failed her first art history exam, and Emily stayed on the phone with her for two whole hours.
all.zh: 米雅從大學打電話來，說她第一次的藝術史考試不及格，艾蜜莉陪她講了整整兩個小時電話。

all.en: Ethan mailed Emily a hand-drawn map of his new neighborhood, with tiny labels marking every cafe that served decent black tea.
all.zh: 伊森寄給艾蜜莉一張手繪的新社區地圖，上面用小標籤標出每一間紅茶還算過得去的咖啡店。

all.en: Emily started a Sunday morning sketch routine, walking the same riverside path her childhood town had also followed.
all.zh: 艾蜜莉養成了週日清晨速寫的習慣，沿著她童年小鎮也曾蜿蜒而過的同一條河邊路徑散步。

all.en: Dad surprised her with a portable easel that folded down small enough to fit inside her university backpack.
all.zh: 爸爸送她一個驚喜禮物，一個折起來小到可以塞進大學書包的攜帶式畫架。

all.en: On her birthday, Mom mailed a tin of homemade pineapple cake with a note that said keep painting, keep eating.
all.zh: 她生日當天，媽媽寄來一盒自製鳳梨酥，附上紙條寫著「繼續畫，繼續吃」。

all.en: Emily taped that note onto the wall of her university studio, right beside the small portrait her first child student had drawn.
all.zh: 艾蜜莉把那張紙條貼在大學工作室的牆上，就在她第一位小學生送的小肖像旁。

all.en: She finally found the courage to ask her mentor whether she could try a residency program in a quiet northern village the following summer.
all.zh: 她終於鼓起勇氣，問導師她能不能在隔年夏天到北部一個寧靜的村莊試試駐村計畫。

all.en: Her mentor smiled and said quietly that home, real home, was a place every artist eventually had to leave at least once to truly understand it.
all.zh: 導師微笑著輕聲說，家，真正的家，是每位藝術家至少必須離開過一次才能真正理解的地方。
